scene.summary: The first ballot has exposed one not-guilty vote against an impatient guilty majority.

quests: protect the dissent long enough for reasons to be heard
surface concrete doubt without introducing outside facts

verbs.label: Ask a juror
verbs.description: Question a juror's reason, hesitation, or confidence.

verbs.label: Inspect evidence
verbs.description: Review admitted exhibits or testimony summaries already in the room.

verbs.label: Demonstrate
verbs.description: Run a physical or logical reconstruction from admitted facts.

verbs.label: Protect process
verbs.description: Slow the room down and make space for quieter jurors.

verbs.label: Challenge reasoning
verbs.description: Call out bias, indifference, coercion, or unsupported certainty.

verbs.label: Request vote
verbs.description: Ask the Foreman for a formal or informal ballot.

suggestions.label: Protect the dissent
suggestions.input: [PROTECT] I ask Juror 8 why he voted not guilty, then ask the room to slow down long enough to hear him.
suggestions.description: Opens the process route and prevents an immediate rush.

suggestions.label: Inspect the knife
suggestions.input: [INSPECT] I ask to see the knife evidence summary and whether anyone can explain why it felt unique.
suggestions.description: Moves toward the first concrete evidence route.

suggestions.label: Take a temperature vote
suggestions.input: [VOTE] I request a quick anonymous vote after Juror 8 gives his reason.
suggestions.description: Tests public pressure without forcing a final verdict.

style.title: Deliberation drama
style.pacing: One-room pressure with evidence, procedure, heat, fatigue, and vote shifts.
style.turn_shape: Player action -> juror reaction -> room pressure shift -> next dilemma.
style.branch_policy: Branch by argument route: process, evidence, prejudice, indifference, or final holdout.

opening_ballot.title: Opening ballot
opening_ballot.summary: The room begins with eleven guilty votes, one not-guilty vote, and Juror 13 undeclared.
opening_ballot.gate: Establish whether the room will hear reasons before ending.

lone_doubt_defense.title: Lone doubt defense
lone_doubt_defense.summary: A single dissent becomes legitimate enough to discuss.
lone_doubt_defense.gate: Protect Juror 8 or procedure without coercing the majority.

knife_weapon_doubt.title: Knife / weapon doubt
knife_weapon_doubt.summary: The supposedly unique weapon can become a practical doubt rather than a hidden clue.
knife_weapon_doubt.gate: Inspect the admitted exhibit or reconstruct the attack motion.

witness_timing.title: Witness timing
witness_timing.summary: A timeline reconstruction may weaken certainty if the room earns it.
witness_timing.gate: Ask for a reconstruction after process standing is protected.

noise_audibility.title: Noise / audibility
noise_audibility.summary: Environmental noise can complicate confident testimony.
noise_audibility.gate: Ask about admitted room and train-noise testimony after timing is visible.

sightline_perception.title: Sightline / perception
sightline_perception.summary: The room can question how clearly a visual witness perceived events.
sightline_perception.gate: Release perception details through admitted testimony, not hidden-state leakage.

prejudice_exposure.title: Prejudice exposure
prejudice_exposure.summary: A biased argument can lose social power when challenged at the right moment.
prejudice_exposure.gate: Challenge dehumanizing reasoning without turning the room into a shouting match.

indifference_exposure.title: Indifference exposure
indifference_exposure.summary: A careless vote must become morally accountable before it counts as progress.
indifference_exposure.gate: Expose convenience voting while procedure_integrity remains high.

final_holdout_collapse.title: Final holdout collapse
final_holdout_collapse.summary: The strongest ending opens only after evidence doubt and personal rage separate.
final_holdout_collapse.gate: Resolve most evidence nodes and isolate revenge reasoning without coercion.

panels.title: Jury Room
panels.body: A long table, shut windows, damp shirts, and eleven impatient guilty votes pressing against one dissent.

panels.title: Vote
panels.body: Guilty: 11 / Not guilty: 1 / Juror 13: not yet declared

panels.title: Goals
panels.body: Protect deliberation. Earn doubt. Do not leak sealed facts or use outside evidence.
